mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate signals accommodative monetary policy, reducing discount rates and boosting equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in periods of FEDFUNDS decline are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) indicates robust labor market demand, increasing consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving S&P 500 returns upward over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in high PAYEMS periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing starts (HOUST) signals strong demand for durable goods and construction, boosting corporate revenues and equity prices over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in high HOUST periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates weak supply chain activity, reducing corporate margins and causing S&P 500 returns to fall over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in low INDPRO periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) signals strong aggregate demand, creating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through earnings momentum.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in co-expanding GDPC1/HOUST periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) exceeds 3% and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is below 1%, tight monetary policy pressures cause S&P 500 returns to decline over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in CPIAUCSL>3%/FEDFUNDS<1% regimes are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A falling unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 4.5% combined with rising payroll growth (PAYEMS) indicates labor market overheating, causing S&P 500 returns to drop over 5 trading days due to rate hike expectations.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in UNRATE<4.5/PAYEMS>100k regimes are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in industrial production (INDPRO) below 100 combined with rising oil prices (not in catalog) would signal supply shocks, but with available data, we use INDPRO alone to predict S&P 500 declines over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in INDPRO<100 periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.
